title: Eternal Dragon requires two Dragon-qualified sacrifices

card.rawText: Requires 2 “Dragon” named or Dragon-type creatures to summon. Place the sacrificed cards under this card while on the field. If this card leaves the field or changes control, the cards under follow it.

plan.title: Eternal Dragon requires two Dragon-qualified sacrifices
plan.summary: Validate summon is allowed only when both sacrifices are Dragon-named or Dragon-type, in addition to AL12 two-sacrifice rule.

notes: Keep at least one player_1 primary creature before summon if possible.
Test valid pair and invalid mixed pair in separate reset runs.

steps: Attempt to summon Eternal Dragon using two Dragon-qualified creatures as sacrifices.
Attempt to summon Eternal Dragon using one Dragon-qualified and one non-Dragon creature.

expectedAssertions.label: Rejected summon leaves Eternal Dragon out of field

manualVerification: Confirm Dragon match accepts either creatureType Dragon or card name containing Dragon.
riskNotes: Runtime support is partial; custom material validation may not exist beyond normal AL sacrifice count.

coverageSuggestion.notes: Plan targets card-specific sacrifice validation without assuming blocker until runtime is checked.

runnerNotes: Create a dev match from plan.setup, execute plan.steps, then verify plan.expectedAssertions.
The LLM only proposes the fixture. The deterministic engine and manual QA status remain source of truth.
